repo_prior_art.cite: stat_ate_overlap_decay + phase bookkeeping for one-sided overlap-tail rates, but for ATE weak-overlap risk rather than a CCOT PRTE endpoint.

published_prior_art.cite: DaouiaFlorensSimar2010FrontierEVT + boundary-tail normalizations for frontier estimators, not CCOT PRTE endpoints; verified at https://arxiv.org/abs/1011.5722.

TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT gives continuous-instrument nonparametric rates, and FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives generic directionally differentiable inference; neither gives a PRTE-OT boundary-tail product experiment.

TanBlanchetSyrgkanis2026PRTEOT Section 5.2 / Proposition 5.9 / Theorem 5.12 gives a plug-in reduction and rate-only continuous-IV result; FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID studies regularized OT inference rather than this unregularized boundary endpoint.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_ate_overlap_decay + overlap-tail minimax-rate machinery, but not PRTE-OT honest-CI adaptation.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2005AdaptiveCI and RobinsVanderVaart2006AdaptiveSets give abstract adaptive-CI/confidence-set lower-bound templates, not a CCOT PRTE two-alpha obstruction.
ImbensManski2004PartialCI and Stoye2009MorePartialCI are regular partial-ID endpoint CI templates; this is only a sanity reduction.
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference and FranguridiLiu2025RegularizedOTPartialID motivate nonsmooth and OT inference but do not construct fixed-alpha honest CIs for this endpoint.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / thm:endpoint-limit-law
novelty_flags.one_line: The weak-overlap inference literature is a closer comparator than the current survey admits; Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats studies thresholded DR inference and Gaussian validity under weak overlap, even though it does not subsume the CCOT PRTE endpoint.

soundness_flags.label: thm:local-experiment
soundness_flags.one_line: With b_n defined by n b_n(P)^2 V_P(b_n(P)) about one, the displayed covariance b_n(P)^2 E[chi_P(O)^2 B^(-1) 1{B>(u vee v)b_n(P)}] is of order 1/n under bounded chi_P, so it does not yield the claimed logarithmic or nondegenerate boundary Gaussian factor.

soundness_flags.label: thm:local-experiment
soundness_flags.one_line: The phase behavior is derived from V_P alone, but no primitive condition ties the chi_P^2-weighted boundary tail to V_P; chi_P could vanish or diverge near B=0 and change the covariance order.

soundness_flags.label: prop:strict-overlap-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Under B >= b_so, the indicator in H_{n,P}(u)=chi_P(O)1{B>u b_n}/B^(1/2) is not zero for small u; strict overlap removes the singular boundary region but does not make the centered process identically vanish.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:active-face-gap
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The limit-law claims are conditional on a unique active OT face with a boundary-scaled dual gap.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The headline inference claims are bound to the unique-active-face subclass P_n(alpha).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-linearization
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular interior component away from the propensity boundary is assumed pathwise differentiable.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The phase-transition claim concerns a boundary factor added to an assumed regular Gaussian interior component.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:boundary-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: A fixed-alpha CI attaining oracle length is explicitly left open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The no-adaptation theorem is only a lower-bound/impossibility claim, not a complete adaptive inference theory.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the boundary-process normalization with one whose covariance has the claimed phase limit, and state the required weighted-tail condition on chi_P explicitly.
Rewrite the strict-overlap reduction so it says the boundary singular term is absent or asymptotically negligible after the corrected normalization, not that H_{n,P} is identically zero.

Add Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats and MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2023DRWeakOverlap as weak-overlap inference comparators, explaining why their thresholded/trimmed DR results do not cover the unregularized CCOT PRTE endpoint.
For thm:no-honest-adaptation, add a named two-point or modulus construction route with the tail exponents, endpoint separation, and bounded-divergence target displayed as proposal objects.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: novelty is plausibly field-level, but the boundary-process scaling and strict-overlap reduction must be repaired before Stage 0.
